question: When will a spacecraft be launched by non-rocket means?

content.text: When will something launch to orbit which is fully or partially lifted by a technology other than rockets or jet engines?
The process involves a stage of at least 1 km vertical ascent, within Earth's atmosphere, that is accomplished by a means other than rockets.
The spacecraft must enter Earth orbit or go beyond Earth.
The spacecraft must survive launch & return telemetry to Earth.
Technologies that are not novel enough to resolve this question by themselves:
Any rockets that work by expelling matter downwards:
Launching from a heavier-than-air plane like the Pegasus rocket does

textDescription:
When will something launch to orbit which is fully or partially lifted by a technology other than rockets or jet engines?

For example:

Rocket launch from lighter-than-air aircraft

Spinlaunch

Space elevator

Launch via railgun

Nuclear pulse propulsion

Over 1km tall launch towers

Details:

The process involves a stage of at least 1 km vertical ascent, within Earth's atmosphere, that is accomplished by a means other than rockets.

The spacecraft must enter Earth orbit or go beyond Earth.

The spacecraft must survive launch & return telemetry to Earth. 

Technologies that are not novel enough to resolve this question by themselves:

Any rockets that work by expelling matter downwards:

Nuclear thermal rockets

Ion engines

Air-breathing rockets

Mountaintop launchpads

Suborbital flight eg Virgin Galactic

Launching from a heavier-than-air plane like the Pegasus rocket does

question: Bonnie Blue beats sexual record on June 15th with 2000+ men?

content.text: This market resolves to Yes if credible media or event organizers publicly confirm that adult performer Bonnie Blue participated in a recorded sexual event on June 15, 2025, involving 2000 or more male participants, and claims to have broken a previous record.
Public documentation or media reporting by at least one reputable outlet, event sponsor, or organizer.
Confirmation that the event took place on June 15, 2025.
Bonnie Blue explicitly claiming or being credited with breaking the record (e.g., “most sexual partners in a day”).
It occurs on a different date.
There is no verifiable public claim of the record.

textDescription:
This market resolves to Yes if credible media or event organizers publicly confirm that adult performer Bonnie Blue participated in a recorded sexual event on June 15, 2025, involving 2000 or more male participants, and claims to have broken a previous record.

Resolution criteria include:

Public documentation or media reporting by at least one reputable outlet, event sponsor, or organizer.

Confirmation that the event took place on June 15, 2025.

Bonnie Blue explicitly claiming or being credited with breaking the record (e.g., “most sexual partners in a day”).

Market resolves to No if:

The event does not occur.

Fewer than 2000 men participate.

It occurs on a different date.

There is no verifiable public claim of the record.

question: AGI When? [High Quality Turing Test]

content.text: This market resolves to the year in which an AI system exists which is capable of passing a high quality, adversarial Turing test. It is used for the Big Clock on the

For proposed testing criteria, refer to
this Metaculus Question by Matthew Barnett,

attrs.class: break-anywhere hover:underline hover:decoration-primary-400 hover:decoration-2 active:underline active:decoration-primary-400

content.text: Longbets wager between Ray Kurzweil and Mitch Kapor.

attrs.class: break-anywhere hover:underline hover:decoration-primary-400 hover:decoration-2 active:underline active:decoration-primary-400

content.text: As of market creation, Metaculus predicts there is an ~

attrs.class: break-anywhere hover:underline hover:decoration-primary-400 hover:decoration-2 active:underline active:decoration-primary-400

content.text: that an AI will pass the Longbets Turing test before 2030, with a median community prediction of July 2028.
Manifold's current prediction of the specific Longbets Turing test can be found here:
This question is intended to determine the Manifold community's median prediction, not just of the Longbets wager specifically but of any similiarly high-quality test.

One or more human judges interview computers and human foils using terminals (so that the judges won't be prejudiced against the computers for lacking a human appearance). The nature of the dialogue between the human judges and the candidates (i.e., the computers and the human foils) is similar to an online chat using instant messaging.

The computers as well as the human foils try to convince the human judges of their humanness. If the human judges are unable to reliably unmask the computers (as imposter humans) then the computer is considered to have demonstrated human-level intelligence.

This question refers to a high quality subset of possible Turing tests that will, in theory, be extremely difficult for any AI to pass if the AI does not possess extensive knowledge of the world, mastery of natural language, common sense, a high level of skill at deception, and the ability to reason at least as well as humans do.
A Turing test is said to be "adversarial" if the human judges make a good-faith attempt, in the best of their abilities, to successfully unmask the AI as an impostor among the participants,

the human confederates make a good-faith attempt, in the best of their abilities, to demonstrate that they are humans. In other words, all of the human participants should be trying to ensure that the AI does not pass the test.
Note: These criteria are still in draft form, and may be updated to better match the spirit of the question. Your feedback is welcome in the comments.

textDescription:
This market resolves to the year in which an AI system exists which is capable of passing a high quality, adversarial Turing test. It is used for the Big Clock on the manifold.markets/ai page.

The Turing test, originally called the imitation game by Alan Turing in 1950, is a test of a machine's ability to exhibit intelligent behaviour equivalent to, or indistinguishable from, that of a human.

For proposed testing criteria, refer to this Metaculus Question by Matthew Barnett, or the Longbets wager between Ray Kurzweil and Mitch Kapor. 


As of market creation, Metaculus predicts there is an ~88% chance that an AI will pass the Longbets Turing test before 2030, with a median community prediction of July 2028.

Manifold's current prediction of the specific Longbets Turing test can be found here:

@/dreev/will-ai-pass-the-turing-test-by-202 

This question is intended to determine the Manifold community's median prediction, not just of the Longbets wager specifically but of any similiarly high-quality test.


Additional Context From Longbets:

One or more human judges interview computers and human foils using terminals (so that the judges won't be prejudiced against the computers for lacking a human appearance). The nature of the dialogue between the human judges and the candidates (i.e., the computers and the human foils) is similar to an online chat using instant messaging. 

The computers as well as the human foils try to convince the human judges of their humanness. If the human judges are unable to reliably unmask the computers (as imposter humans) then the computer is considered to have demonstrated human-level intelligence.

Additional Context From Metaculus:

This question refers to a high quality subset of possible Turing tests that will, in theory, be extremely difficult for any AI to pass if the AI does not possess extensive knowledge of the world, mastery of natural language, common sense, a high level of skill at deception, and the ability to reason at least as well as humans do. 

A Turing test is said to be "adversarial" if the human judges make a good-faith attempt, in the best of their abilities, to successfully unmask the AI as an impostor among the participants, and the human confederates make a good-faith attempt, in the best of their abilities, to demonstrate that they are humans. In other words, all of the human participants should be trying to ensure that the AI does not pass the test.

Note: These criteria are still in draft form, and may be updated to better match the spirit of the question. Your feedback is welcome in the comments.

question: Golf: Will the winner of the 2025 Men's U.S. Open finish under par?

content.text: The 2025 Men's U.S. Open will be held at Oakmont Country Club from June 12th through 15th, 2025. The U.S. Open is traditionally set up to make scoring very difficult with fast greens and long, thick rough and this year looks to be no different. Will the winner of this year's tournament post a cumulative 4-round score of under par (-1 or lower)?
The unlikely event of a playoff at even par will be resolved as a "No" as the playoff is not considered for the player's official score.

textDescription:
The 2025 Men's U.S. Open will be held at Oakmont Country Club from June 12th through 15th, 2025. The U.S. Open is traditionally set up to make scoring very difficult with fast greens and long, thick rough and this year looks to be no different. Will the winner of this year's tournament post a cumulative 4-round score of under par (-1 or lower)?

The unlikely event of a playoff at even par will be resolved as a "No" as the playoff is not considered for the player's official score.

question: Will Max Verstappen and George Russell collide during the Canadian Grand Prix?

content.text: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Max Verstappen and George Russell make contact during the 2025 Canadian Grand Prix. This contact does not have to result in significant damage or a driver retirement to qualify for “Yes”. If no notable contact is made, the market will resolve to “No”. The FIA race incident log and live race coverage will be considered to determine the outcome. A collision can occur at any point in the race, not just lap 1 or turn 1.
The 2025 Canadian Grand Prix is scheduled for June 15, 2025, at the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve in Montreal. George Russell secured pole position for the race, with Max Verstappen qualifying second. (

In the previous Spanish Grand Prix, Verstappen and Russell were involved in a collision that resulted in a 10-second penalty for Verstappen and three penalty points on his super-licence, bringing him one point away from a race ban. (
) Despite this, Verstappen has stated he will not alter his aggressive racing style. (
The Circuit Gilles Villeneuve is known for its tight corners and close racing, which can increase the likelihood of on-track incidents.

Given their recent on-track clashes and the competitive nature of both drivers, there is heightened anticipation regarding their interactions during the Canadian Grand Prix. Traders should consider Verstappen's proximity to a race ban and how this might influence his approach to the race.

textDescription:
Resolution criteria

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Max Verstappen and George Russell make contact during the 2025 Canadian Grand Prix. This contact does not have to result in significant damage or a driver retirement to qualify for “Yes”. If no notable contact is made, the market will resolve to “No”. The FIA race incident log and live race coverage will be considered to determine the outcome. A collision can occur at any point in the race, not just lap 1 or turn 1. 

Background

The 2025 Canadian Grand Prix is scheduled for June 15, 2025, at the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve in Montreal. George Russell secured pole position for the race, with Max Verstappen qualifying second. (en.wikipedia.org)

In the previous Spanish Grand Prix, Verstappen and Russell were involved in a collision that resulted in a 10-second penalty for Verstappen and three penalty points on his super-licence, bringing him one point away from a race ban. (reuters.com) Despite this, Verstappen has stated he will not alter his aggressive racing style. (reuters.com)

The Circuit Gilles Villeneuve is known for its tight corners and close racing, which can increase the likelihood of on-track incidents.

Considerations

Given their recent on-track clashes and the competitive nature of both drivers, there is heightened anticipation regarding their interactions during the Canadian Grand Prix. Traders should consider Verstappen's proximity to a race ban and how this might influence his approach to the race.
